changes.detail: All five mandatory front matter fields present: work_id, branch (work/BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1-renal-signal-authority-and-reusable-collision-model), risk_level (HIGH), execution_model (TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH), change_type (MIXED). No field missing.

HIGH is correct. Sprint may interact with signal_evaluator.py (Intelligence Core) if minimal runtime anti-double-counting support is required. Any Intelligence Core interaction = HIGH by default regardless of file path (SOP §10). MIXED covers governance artefact creation (CONTENT) plus potential runtime authority wiring (BEHAVIOUR). MIXED is governed by BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4.

TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH is correct. Phase 1 = investigation and design report. Phase 2 = governance model creation + eGFR activation decision. A separate human STOP gate (READY_FOR_HUMAN_STOP_GATE + APPROVE BATCH2 EGFR AUTHORITY ACTIVATION) guards any runtime activation beyond Phase 2. This is structurally sound.

Package exists. signal_id: signal_egfr_low. Activation key: signal_egfr_low::inv_egfr_low_chronic_kidney_function_reduction. Frame: frame_egfr_pass3_chronic_kidney_function_reduction. promotion_state: compiled_not_promoted. runtime_authority_status: inactive. collision_status: requires_adjudication. clinical_adjudication_status: required_before_activation.

Package exists. signal_id: signal_egfr_low. Activation key: signal_egfr_low::inv_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop. Frame: frame_egfr_pass3_hemodynamic_filtration_drop. promotion_state: compiled_not_promoted. runtime_authority_status: inactive. collision_status: requires_adjudication. ROUTE_C multi-frame preserved (notes line 1084-1085).

File exists. Both eGFR packages listed at lines 314-365. pkg_kb47_egfr_low_chronic_kidney_function_reduction: blocker_type=renal_authority_adjudication_required, final_recommendation=REQUIRES_ARCHITECTURE_AUTHORITY_DECISION (line 341). pkg_kb47_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop: blocker_type=anti_double_counting_required, same final recommendation (line 365). Recommended next action for both: BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1 (lines 340, 364).

File exists. signal_egfr_low family starts at line 1033. Two frames indexed: chronic (line 1039) and hemodynamic (line 1065). Both have collision_group_id and collision_status: requires_adjudication. Both promotion_state: compiled_not_promoted. Both runtime_authority_status: inactive.

File exists. Key finding: activation_decision.summary: no_runtime_activation_no_retirement_no_package_modification (line 133). runtime_activation_allowed: false (line 138). Note at line 53: 'eGFR is a corroborator not a replacement frame' within creatinine authority. Legacy s24 frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_egfr_escalation (runtime_active_legacy_unadjudicated) uses override rule or_renal_ckd_stage_3 for eGFR<60 — directly relevant to the collision the sprint must address.

File exists. egfr group: outcome=KEEP_BLOCKED, blocker=renal_authority_and_anti_double_counting_required (line 38). Recommended next action: BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1 architecture adjudication before promotion/activation (line 39). Notes confirm no anti-double-counting mechanism in evaluator (line 41). This is the current authoritative formal blocker state.
File exists. eGFR packages were excluded from BATCH2-PROMOTE-1; this is cross-referenced in batch2_remainder_resolution_register_v1.yaml line 328. Both eGFR packages remain never-promoted.

File exists. CF-BATCH2-007 at line 70: Status=Resolved. CRITICAL INTERPRETATION: 'Resolved' means BATCH2-REMAINING-BLOCKERS-1 recorded the formal blocker — NOT that eGFR is activated. The carry-forward item (adjudication task) is resolved; the eGFR blocking condition is NOT resolved. Both eGFR packages remain compiled_not_promoted_inactive. Cursor must not misread CF-BATCH2-007 as resolved-equals-active.

File exists. SignalRegistry loads all packages/*/signal_library.yaml. Only inter-signal logic is a hard error on duplicate activation_key (lines 59-64: 'Duplicate activation_key collision' ValueError). NO collision suppression, NO authority priority, NO signal-family-level anti-double-counting mechanism exists. This confirms the governance-only default approach in the prompt is correct — any runtime anti-double-counting requires new Intelligence Core code.

creatinine_multiframe_authority_decision_v1.yaml explicitly states eGFR is 'a corroborator not a replacement frame' within creatinine authority (line 53). The legacy frame frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_egfr_escalation is runtime_active_legacy_unadjudicated (index line 43, runtime_authority_status: active at line 25 of creatinine decision). Activating signal_egfr_low without anti-double-counting would present two independent renal-filtration signals. The sprint correctly identifies this as the core risk.

SignalEvaluator has no collision resolution, no signal family priority, no suppression path. Only inter-signal logic is the hard duplicate activation_key error in SignalRegistry._load(). This validates that runtime anti-double-counting requires new code in Intelligence Core — correctly HIGH risk and correctly deferred behind a human STOP gate.

BATCH2-REMAINING-BLOCKERS-1: status=analysis_complete_ready_for_human_stop_gate (line 3). runtime_code_changed: false (line 17). activated_package_count: 0 (line 15). No eGFR activation was performed. eGFR blocker formally recorded as renal_authority_and_anti_double_counting_required. This is the verified starting state for BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1.

Prompt explicitly prohibits modification of: signal_library.yaml, research_brief.yaml, thresholds, clinical wording, frontend, SSOT, scoring, report compiler — unless STOP-level justification with explicit statement. Prohibition list is comprehensive and unambiguous. STOP conditions protect the boundary.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 236-243 (governance-only default); lines 332-343 (STOP gate before activation); lines 373-385 (regression tests if runtime changed)
changes.detail: Behavioural scope is well-bounded: (1) signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml is governance-only by default (runtime_consumed: false mandatory). (2) Runtime implementation is conditional on meeting strict criteria: minimal, reusable, fail-safe. (3) STOP gate (READY_FOR_HUMAN_STOP_GATE + explicit approval phrase) is required before any runtime activation. (4) If broad redesign needed, STOP is mandatory. (5) Six regression test targets defined for runtime path; tests-not-required path for governance-only is also explicit.

If runtime anti-double-counting implemented: 6 tests required covering eGFR emission, creatinine no-duplicate, creatinine supporting evidence, distinct acute layers, unrelated signals unaffected, inactive without approval. If governance-only (expected path): tests required only for governance validators if added. Coverage is adequate for both paths.

Primary artefact runtime_consumed: false is mandatory. No analytical output can change from governance-only creation. Determinism is only at risk if runtime anti-double-counting code is added to signal_evaluator.py — gated by human STOP approval. The prompt correctly isolates and gates this risk.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 93-103 (baseline STOP); lines 256-264 (Phase 1 STOP); lines 329-343 (activation STOP gate)
changes.detail: Baseline STOP covers: branch not main, local main != origin/main, dirty tree, missing medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml, missing batch2_remainder_resolution_register_v1.yaml, eGFR packages not found, creatinine authority/frames not found. Phase 1 STOP covers: relationship unresolvable, duplicate signalling risk, runtime requires broad redesign, rollback path undefined. Activation STOP gate requires explicit READY_FOR_HUMAN_STOP_GATE report then APPROVE BATCH2 EGFR AUTHORITY ACTIVATION. Complete and appropriately placed.

signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml is a new artefact not duplicating any existing authority source. Creatinine authority remains in creatinine_multiframe_authority_decision_v1.yaml. Medical frame authority remains in medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml. No duplicate authority source risk.

All commands use python backend/scripts/... and git invocations. No bash-specific syntax. Validation scripts (run_architecture_validation_gate.py, validate_medical_frame_identity_index.py, validate_context_modifier_catalogue.py, validate_knowledge_package.py) are Python invocations compatible with Windows PowerShell.

CF-BATCH2-007 shows Status: 'Resolved'. A hasty reader could interpret this as eGFR being resolved/active. HARDENING INSTRUCTION: Resolved means the adjudication-task carry-forward item was resolved by BATCH2-REMAINING-BLOCKERS-1 recording the formal blocker. Both eGFR packages remain compiled_not_promoted_inactive. Cursor must verify eGFR package runtime_authority_status via medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml lines 1047-1048 and 1073-1074 before proceeding — not rely on CF-BATCH2-007 status interpretation.

Source: batch2_remaining_blockers_execution_register_v1.yaml lines 3-17.

hardening_notes: One warning issued: CF-BATCH2-007 'Resolved' status must not be misread as eGFR activation. Cursor must verify eGFR package state from medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml directly. All authority paths verified from source content. signal_evaluator.py confirmed to have NO collision/suppression mechanism — governance-only default approach is correct and safe. No blockers found. Sprint may proceed to Stage 3 (python backend/scripts/run_work_package.py start).
